footer.platform: TRIZ Technology - Pedagogical Creative Thinking Platform

lessons.name: Introduction to TRIZ: History and Fundamental Principles
lessons.description: TRIZ (Theory of Inventive Problem Solving) is a scientific and systematic approach to solving complex problems creatively. This course provides comprehensive information about the historical origins of TRIZ, its creator Genrikh Altshuller, and his foundational ideas. TRIZ was created in 1956 and has since been adopted by leading companies worldwide.
lessons.methodology: Historical analysis, Text analysis, Conceptual models
lessons.applications: Creative thinking, Problem solving, Technical innovation

lessons.name: Problems and Contradictory Requirements: TRIZ's Core Component
lessons.description: Technical problems are composed of contradictory requirements. TRIZ's contradiction matrix (39x39) is a mathematically structured tool where each row represents a parameter to be improved, and each column represents the undesired effect of that improvement. This course details TRIZ's most powerful tool.
lessons.methodology: Contradiction matrix analysis, Parameter analysis, Solution selection
lessons.applications: Technical problem solving, Parameter optimization, Innovative design

lessons.name: TRIZ's 40 Inventive Principles and Their Practical Applications
lessons.description: The 40 inventive principles were developed by analyzing hundreds of patents. Principles like 'Segmentation,' 'Combination,' and 'Multifunctionality' are explained with practical examples. This course details all 40 principles and their physical foundations.
lessons.methodology: Principle catalog, Example analysis, Practical application
lessons.applications: Finding inventive solutions, Technical innovation, Problem solving

lessons.name: Idealization Concept and Ideal Final Result (IFR)
lessons.description: One of TRIZ's most important ideas is system idealization. Ideal Final Result is a concept where the problem solves itself. This course teaches the IFR concept and how it guides engineers toward correct solutions.
lessons.methodology: Idealization method, System analysis, Goal setting
lessons.applications: Goal clarification, Strategy development, Resource optimization

lessons.name: Resource Analysis and Optimal Utilization Strategies
lessons.description: Every system has available resources. TRIZ categorizes resources into system resources and environmental resources. Many problems can be solved by reorganizing existing resources without adding new ones. This course details resource analysis and optimal utilization strategies.
lessons.methodology: Resource catalog, Potential analysis, Optimization
lessons.applications: Cost reduction, Efficient utilization, Improvement

lessons.name: System Evolution Laws and Improvement Trends
lessons.description: TRIZ identifies eight fundamental improvement trends that systems follow. Understanding these trends enables prediction and prevention of problems. Students learn to analyze trends in their field and forecast future changes.
lessons.methodology: Historical analysis, Trend analysis, Forecasting
lessons.applications: Future development prediction, Strategic planning, Improvement pathway

lessons.name: Problem Schemes (Functional Analysis) and System Modeling
lessons.description: To understand complex systems, it's essential to identify all functions and represent them visually. Functional Analysis (FAST) is a method of diagramming each system component's role and interactions. Once the problem scheme is created, other TRIZ tools become more effective.
lessons.methodology: Function analysis, FAST method, Diagram creation
lessons.applications: System decomposition, Problem structure, Componentization

lessons.name: Time Factor, Phase Separation, and Change Mechanisms
lessons.description: Systems are dynamic and change over time. Time plays an important role in TRIZ. A single problem's solution can be implemented at different time phases. This course teaches time analysis, rhythmic analysis, and phase separation principles.
lessons.methodology: Time analysis, Rhythmic analysis, Phase separation
lessons.applications: Dynamic problems, Change mechanisms, Time scheduling

lessons.name: Solutions at Micro and Macro Scales and Scaling
lessons.description: Some problems are solved at the micro-scale while others are solved at the macro-scale. TRIZ recommends combining both scales. This course teaches solutions at various scales and their advantages and limitations.
lessons.methodology: Scale analysis, Multi-level approach, Hierarchical models
lessons.applications: Nanotechnology, Macro design, System integration

lessons.name: Information Transfer Between Technologies and Domains
lessons.description: TRIZ's greatest strength is applying solutions from one field to another. Biomimicry - technologies inspired by nature - is central to this concept. This course teaches analyzing solutions from different fields and transferring them.
lessons.methodology: Interdisciplinary analysis, Solution transfer, Comparative studies
lessons.applications: Innovation practice, Cross-domain synthesis, Biomimicry

lessons.name: Prototyping, Rapid Experimentation, and Iterative Improvement
lessons.description: Implementing solutions often requires prototyping. TRIZ-based rapid experimentation and iterative improvement are essential. This course teaches prototyping stages, testing methodologies, and rapid iteration strategies aligned with Agile and Lean methodologies.
lessons.methodology: Prototyping methodology, Rapid iteration, Experiment analysis
lessons.applications: Practical implementation, Product improvement, Market introduction

lessons.name: Risks, Constraints, and Ethical Issues in TRIZ
lessons.description: Any solution carries risks, regulatory constraints, and ethical considerations. TRIZ acknowledges these aspects. This course teaches risk analysis, legal constraints, and finding solutions that are both creative and responsible.
lessons.methodology: Risk analysis, Ethical reasoning, Legal constraints
lessons.applications: Responsible improvement, Ethical innovation, Standards compliance

lessons.name: TRIZ Software and Computer Tools
lessons.description: Modern TRIZ is implemented through specialized software tools like iGrafx TRIZ and TRIZ Master. This course teaches famous TRIZ software, their functions, and applications. Students learn using computer tools to find solutions efficiently.
lessons.methodology: Software tools, Computer simulation, Algorithm analysis
lessons.applications: Automated analysis, Fast solution finding, Database utilization

lessons.name: Applying TRIZ in Engineering: Mechanical Engineering and Mechanics
lessons.description: Mechanical engineering is a primary TRIZ application domain. This course teaches how automotive design, electric motors, and mechanical systems are solved using TRIZ. Students analyze real mechanical problems and solve them using TRIZ.
lessons.methodology: Technical analysis, Design methodology, Simulation
lessons.applications: Automotive industry, Mechanical systems, Energy sector

lessons.name: Applying TRIZ in Biology, Medicine, and Pharmacology
lessons.description: TRIZ applies to biology and medicine. Drugs must be effective but without harmful side effects - a contradiction. Medical devices and surgical mechanisms are improved using TRIZ. This course teaches solving medical problems using systematic TRIZ analysis.
lessons.methodology: Biology analysis, Medical problems, Experimental methods
lessons.applications: Drug development, Medical technologies, Biological improvement

lessons.name: Applying TRIZ in Electronics, Computers, and Software
lessons.description: Electronics and IT are emerging TRIZ application domains. Processor design involves contradictions like speed vs heat. Complex software must run fast - another contradiction. This course teaches applying TRIZ to IT problems effectively.
lessons.methodology: IT analysis, Algorithm optimization, Architecture improvement
lessons.applications: Chip design, Software optimization, Algorithm improvement

lessons.name: Collaborative Creative Work and TRIZ Brainstorming
lessons.description: Organizing groups using TRIZ requires careful team structure. This course teaches TRIZ-focused brainstorming, team dynamics, and creating creative atmospheres. Well-trained teams find more creative and effective solutions.
lessons.methodology: Team dynamics, Brainstorming, Discussion facilitation
lessons.applications: Problem-solving teams, Corporate innovation, Research laboratories

lessons.name: TRIZ and the Future: Artificial Intelligence, Robotics, and Automation
lessons.description: TRIZ can play a key role in addressing future challenges. This course teaches future TRIZ applications, improving AI systems through TRIZ, and solving robotics problems. Students explore how TRIZ enables humans to maintain control over increasingly complex systems.
lessons.methodology: Future forecasting, AI analysis, Strategic planning
lessons.applications: AI improvement, Robotics, Automated systems

lessons.name: TRIZ Certification and Professional Licensing
lessons.description: TRIZ professionals' competence is validated through official certifications. TRIZ certification has four levels: Level 1 - Basic through Level 4 - Expert. This course prepares students for certification and guides professional development.
lessons.methodology: Certification standards, Exam preparation, Professional development
lessons.applications: Professional licensing, Certification, Professional practice

lessons.name: Practical Projects and Real-World TRIZ Solutions
lessons.description: In this final course, students select a real problem and solve it completely using TRIZ methodology. Students perform problem analysis, select TRIZ principles, apply contradiction matrices, build prototypes, and present results. These projects transform theoretical knowledge into practical expertise.
lessons.methodology: Practical application, Project management, Result presentation
lessons.applications: Real solutions, Corporate innovation, Problem solving
